# agentic-playbooks

> A local runner for agentic playbooks: YAML playbook definitions, a svelte-flow web visualizer, semver versioning, and an MCP server so coding agents can drive playbooks. Rust workspace, edition 2024. The binary is called `apb`.

The MCP server (started with `apb mcp`) identifies itself as `agentic-playbooks` to MCP clients. The binary name and all CLI commands use `apb`.

## Install

Prefer the one-liner installer:

```sh
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -LsSf https://github.com/itechmeat/agentic-playbooks/releases/latest/download/apb-installer.sh | sh
```

This installs the `apb` binary to `CARGO_HOME` (`~/.cargo/bin` by default), no
build tools required.

Fall back to a source build only if the installer is not usable in your
environment (for example, no network access to GitHub Releases). This
requires Rust (stable) and Bun; the web frontend must be built before the Rust
binary because it is embedded at compile time via rust-embed.

```sh
git clone https://github.com/itechmeat/agentic-playbooks && cd agentic-playbooks
(cd web && bun install && bun run build)
cargo install --path crates/apb-cli
```

`cargo install --git` is not supported: git mode builds in a private cargo cache where `web/dist` cannot be placed. Install from a local clone (`--path`).

Verify the installation:

```sh
apb --version
apb doctor
```

## Connect to a coding agent (MCP)

`apb mcp` starts a stdio MCP server on the current project's `.apb/` directory. Use the name `agentic-playbooks` when registering it.

Claude Code (project scope):

```sh
claude mcp add agentic-playbooks -- apb mcp
```

Claude Code (global scope):

```sh
claude mcp add --scope user agentic-playbooks -- apb mcp
```

Project `.mcp.json` (shareable, commit to repo):

```json
{ "mcpServers": { "agentic-playbooks": { "command": "apb", "args": ["mcp"] } } }
```

Codex (`~/.codex/config.toml`):

```toml
[mcp_servers.agentic-playbooks]
command = "apb"
args = ["mcp"]
```

OpenCode (`~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, mcp section):

```json
"agentic-playbooks": {
  "type": "local",
  "command": ["apb", "mcp"],
  "enabled": true
}
```

Run `apb init` in each project where you want to use playbooks. The MCP server discovers `.apb/` relative to the current working directory.

## CLI commands

```text
apb init            create the .apb structure
apb list            playbooks and versions
apb validate        validate playbook schema
apb run <id>        run a playbook
apb runs            list runs
apb resume <run>    resume a paused or interrupted run
apb review          decide a pending human_review node
apb serve           web UI on port 7321
apb mcp             stdio MCP server for coding agents
apb doctor          diagnose environment
apb detect          detect installed coding agents
apb profile         manage agent profiles
apb migrate         migrate schema 1 playbooks to schema 2
```

## Architecture

Five crates under `crates/` with dependency direction core <- engine <- mcp, and cli and server on top:

- `apb-core`: domain layer (no async). Playbook schema, validator, profiles, registry, versioning, trust store, atomic state IO.
- `apb-engine`: execution. Drive loop, node execution, immutable run manifest, agent adapters, append-only event log, background supervisor.
- `apb-mcp`: rmcp stdio MCP server, policy gate, profile and supervisor tools.
- `apb-cli`: package name `apb` (bin `apb`); thin dispatch.
- `apb-server`: axum web API with the svelte frontend embedded via rust-embed.
